An Additional Uncertainty Due to the Need For
Extrapolation of Angular Distributions from O0454002

Figure 3.
After a Bombardment the Active Catcher Foil was
Removed from the Target Chamber and Placed Under a
Calibrated, Thin-Wall Geiger Counter. The Time Required
For this Operation was Long Enough to Permit the
Complete Decay of any F-17 Activity Due to O-16(P,G)
Reaction.
Target Mounting Clamp Modified So That a 1 Mg/Cm**2
Aluminum Foil,1.0-In. Wide and 1.5-Ins. High,Could be
Held at a Distance of 0.025-In. From the Target Foil
In The Forward Direction.

Relative Uncertainty.
The Uncertainty of Cross Section Scale Due to
Target Conditions and the Uncertainty in the Absolute
Efficiency of the Positron Counter.
- By Author - The Authors Determined the Fraction of
F-18 Atoms Which Reached the Aluminum Catcher Foil, the
Fraction Which Remained in the Target Foil and the
Fraction Recoiling in the Backward Direction.
The Resulting Alpha-Particle Yield at 90 Degree was
Used to Confirm the Condition of the Target and to
Check the Consistency of the Energy Calibration of the
Machine by Comparison with Our Previous (P,ALPHA) Data.
